timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-06-20. Aggregated across CNN's OpenAI-board-crisis coverage (no single CNN article carries 15+ dated entries). Contributing articles: https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/17/tech/openai-ceo-sam-altman-fired ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/17/tech/openai-greg-brockman-resigns ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/18/tech/openai-altman-investors-board ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/19/tech/openai-altman-talks-stall ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/20/tech/microsoft-altman-brockman-ai ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/20/tech/openai-emmett-shear-interim-ceo ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/20/tech/openai-employee-letter-resignation-threat ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/21/tech/openai-sam-altman-returns ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/22/tech/openai-q-star-letter-board ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/11/29/tech/openai-altman-formally-returns ; https://www.cnn.com/2023/12/08/tech/microsoft-openai-board-observer ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/08/tech/openai-board-altman-investigation ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/05/14/tech/ilya-sutskever-leaves-openai ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/05/17/tech/openai-jan-leike-superalignment ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/25/tech/openai-mira-murati-departure ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/02/tech/openai-funding-round-157-billion . Date approximations per rule 4: Several entries (Nov 17 Brockman resignation, Nov 17 Murati interim appointment) share a single calendar date because the announcements were sequential within hours; ordering preserved as reported. Nov 20 entries (Microsoft offer, Shear interim, employee letter) similarly all happened on the same fast-moving day. Source disagreements: Reuters' Nov 22 Q* reporting was framed by CNN as 'staff researchers warned the board' — OpenAI never formally confirmed the Q* details; entry written cautiously to attribute to reporting rather than confirmed fact. Public figures named: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Mira Murati, Ilya Sutskever, Jan Leike, Emmett Shear, Nicole Seligman, Satya Nadella — all public corporate officers, permitted under rule 6.

Fetched 2026-06-20. Primary anchor source is AJ's 'OpenAI's board coup: A timeline of the Altman saga' (Nov 22 2023). Supplemented with later AJ reporting to extend the timeline beyond the Nov 22 anchor: https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/11/17/openai-says-sam-altman-out-as-ceo-after-board-loses-confidence ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/11/20/microsoft-hires-ousted-openai-ceo-sam-altman ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/11/20/openai-staff-threaten-to-quit-en-masse-unless-board-resigns ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/11/21/openai-says-sam-altman-to-return-as-ceo ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2023/11/29/sam-altman-formally-returns-as-openai-ceo ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2024/3/9/openai-board-says-altmans-removal-was-not-warranted ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2024/5/15/openai-co-founder-ilya-sutskever-leaves-the-firm ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2024/5/18/jan-leike-quits-openai-saying-safety-took-a-back-seat ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2024/9/26/openai-cto-mira-murati-says-she-is-leaving-the-company ; https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2024/10/3/openai-raises-66bn-at-157bn-valuation . Date approximations per rule 4: Nov 18 'rival venture' discussions (AJ source: Bloomberg/Reuters wire) pegged to weekend reporting; Nov 22 Q* surfacing pegged to Reuters wire date as carried by AJ. WilmerHale review release dated Mar 8 2024 (AJ published Mar 9; the report was released the prior day). Source disagreements: Board's stated reason for firing — AJ used the original board-statement language 'not consistently candid'; CNN paraphrased as 'loss of confidence'. Both are attributable to OpenAI's Nov 17 statement. Sutskever's Nov 20 'regret' tweet — AJ included it as a discrete event; CNN folded it into the broader Nov 20 employee-letter narrative. Public figures named: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Mira Murati, Ilya Sutskever, Jan Leike, Satya Nadella — all public corporate officers, permitted under rule 6.

disagreements.note: AJ quotes the OpenAI board's original 'not consistently candid' phrasing verbatim; CNN paraphrases as 'loss of confidence'. Both phrasings trace to the same Nov 17 2023 board statement.

On Nov 22 2023 Reuters reported that OpenAI researchers had written to the board about a project called Q* prior to Altman's firing. OpenAI did not confirm the reporting. Both timelines carry the entry but frame it as reporting/surfacing rather than confirmed fact.
